arguments.file_text:
# HelpChatbot — Blazor RAG Chatbot Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor Server that answers customer questions about internal product PDF documentation. Uses a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline with Postgres + pgvector for vector storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF source files.

## Technology Selection (per skill guidance)

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| UI | Blazor Server (Interactive) | C# full-stack, real-time streaming |
| LLM abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic, DI-friendly |
| LLM provider | OpenAI (swappable) | Common default; user configures API key |
| Vector store | pgvector via `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` + `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData` | User requested Postgres — pgvector adds vector similarity search |
| PDF parsing | `UglyToad.PdfPig` | MIT-licensed, pure .NET PDF text extraction |
| Embeddings | `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>` (MEAI) | Same abstraction layer as chat |
| ORM / data | EF Core + Npgsql | Postgres schema management, chat history |

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐    ┌────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐
│ Blazor UI    │───▶│ ChatService│───▶│ IChatClient  │──▶ OpenAI / LLM
│ (Chat.razor) │    │            │    └─────────────┘
└──────────────┘    │            │    ┌─────────────┐
                    │            │───▶│ VectorSearch │──▶ pgvector
                    └────────────┘    └─────────────┘
                    ┌────────────┐
                    │DocIngestion│───▶ PDF ➜ Chunk ➜ Embed ➜ pgvector
                    └────────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & NuGet packages
Add all required NuGet packages to `HelpChatbot.csproj`:
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` + `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` (MEAI abstraction + OpenAI provider)
- `OpenAI` (provider SDK)
- `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` (vector store abstraction)
- `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` (EF Core Postgres)
- `UglyToad.PdfPig` (PDF text extraction)
- `Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers` (client-side token counting)

### 2. Configuration & secrets
- Add `appsettings.json` sections for AI options (model ID, embedding model) and Postgres connection string.
- Use `IOptions<T>` pattern. API keys come from environment variables / user-secrets — never committed.

### 3. Data model & EF Core DbContext
- `ChatSession` — conversation metadata (Id, Title, CreatedAt)
- `ChatMessage` — individual messages (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `DocumentChunk` — embedded doc chunks (Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding vector)
- `AppDbContext` with pgvector column type for embeddings

### 4. PDF ingestion service (`DocIngestionService`)
- On startup (or on-demand), scan `Docs/` folder for PDFs.
- Extract text with PdfPig, split into semantic chunks (~500 tokens each, paragraph-aware).
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator`.
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into `DocumentChunks` table. Skip already-ingested files (hash check).

### 5. Vector search service (`VectorSearchService`)
- Accept a user query, embed it, and perform cosine similarity search against `DocumentChunks`.
- Return top-K chunks above a minimum relevance threshold (0.75).
- Include source attribution (file name, chunk index).

### 6. Chat service (`ChatService`)
- Orchestrate the RAG flow: embed query → retrieve context → build prompt → call LLM → return answer.
- System prompt instructs the LLM to answer only from provided context and cite sources.
- Persist messages to `ChatMessages` table.
- Set temperature=0, explicit max tokens, retry logic.

### 7. Blazor UI (`Chat.razor`)
- Chat interface with message history, input box, send button.
- Stream LLM responses token-by-token via `IAsyncEnumerable`.
- Display source citations below each assistant response.
- Simple session management (new chat / continue).

### 8. DI registration & Program.cs
- Register `IChatClient`, `IEmbeddingGenerator`, DbContext, and application services.
- Configure Blazor Server interactive mode.
- Add health check for OpenAI + Postgres.

### 9. Validation & testing
- `dotnet build` clean with no warnings.
- Verify PDF ingestion parses and chunks the sample `product-guide.pdf`.
- Verify end-to-end chat flow returns grounded answers.

## Key Guardrails (from skill guidance)
- Temperature = 0 for factual Q&A
- Pin model versions (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- Retry with exponential backoff on LLM calls
- Minimum similarity score threshold on vector search
- Source attribution on every answer
- API keys from env vars / user-secrets only
- `IOptions<T>` for all configuration
- Streaming responses for UX
- Token counting with `Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers`

## Notes
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf` — ingestion will process this on first run.
- Postgres with pgvector extension must be available. Connection string is configured in `appsettings.json`.
- The OpenAI API key must be set via `AI__ApiKey` environment variable or `dotnet user-secrets`.

# HelpChatbot — Blazor RAG Chatbot Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor Server that answers customer questions about internal product PDF documentation. Uses a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline with Postgres + pgvector for vector storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF source files.

## Technology Selection (per skill guidance)

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| UI | Blazor Server (Interactive) | C# full-stack, real-time streaming |
| LLM abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic, DI-friendly |
| LLM provider | OpenAI (swappable) | Common default; user configures API key |
| Vector store | pgvector via `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` + `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData` | User requested Postgres — pgvector adds vector similarity search |
| PDF parsing | `UglyToad.PdfPig` | MIT-licensed, pure .NET PDF text extraction |
| Embeddings | `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>` (MEAI) | Same abstraction layer as chat |
| ORM / data | EF Core + Npgsql | Postgres schema management, chat history |

## Architecture

```

- `ChatSession` — conversation metadata (Id, Title, CreatedAt)

# HelpChatbot — Blazor RAG Chatbot Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor Server that answers customer questions about internal product PDF documentation. Uses a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline with Postgres + pgvector for vector storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF source files.

## Technology Selection

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| UI | Blazor Server (Interactive) | C# full-stack, real-time streaming |
| LLM abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic, DI-friendly |
| LLM provider | OpenAI (swappable) | Common default; user configures API key |
| Vector store | pgvector via Npgsql + `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData` | User requested Postgres — pgvector adds vector similarity search |
| PDF parsing | `UglyToad.PdfPig` | MIT-licensed, pure .NET PDF text extraction |
| Embeddings | `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>` (MEAI) | Same abstraction layer as chat |
| ORM / data | EF Core + Npgsql | Postgres schema management, chat history |

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐    ┌────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐
│ Blazor UI    │───▶│ ChatService│───▶│ IChatClient  │──▶ OpenAI / LLM
│ (Chat.razor) │    │            │    └─────────────┘
└──────────────┘    │            │    ┌─────────────┐
                    │            │───▶│ VectorSearch │──▶ pgvector
                    └────────────┘    └─────────────┘
                    ┌────────────┐
                    │DocIngestion│───▶ PDF → Chunk → Embed → pgvector
                    └────────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & NuGet packages
Add all required NuGet packages to `HelpChatbot.csproj`:
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` + `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI`
- `OpenAI` (provider SDK)
- `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions`
- `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL`
- `UglyToad.PdfPig` (PDF text extraction)
- `Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers` (client-side token counting)

### 2. Configuration & secrets
- Add `appsettings.json` with AI options (model ID, embedding model) and Postgres connection string.
- Use `IOptions<T>` pattern. API keys via environment variables / user-secrets only.

### 3. Data model & EF Core DbContext
- `ChatSession` — conversation metadata (Id, Title, CreatedAt)
- `ChatMessage` — individual messages (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `DocumentChunk` — embedded doc chunks (Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding vector)
- `AppDbContext` with pgvector column type for embeddings

### 4. PDF ingestion service (`DocIngestionService`)
- Scan `Docs/` folder for PDFs on startup or on-demand.
- Extract text with PdfPig, split into semantic chunks (~500 tokens, paragraph-aware).
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator`.
- Upsert chunks into `DocumentChunks` table. Skip already-ingested files (content hash check).

### 5. Vector search service (`VectorSearchService`)
- Embed user query, cosine similarity search against `DocumentChunks`.
- Return top-5 chunks above minimum relevance threshold (0.75).
- Include source attribution (file name, chunk index).

### 6. Chat service (`ChatService`)
- RAG flow: embed query → retrieve context → build prompt → call LLM → stream answer.
- System prompt: answer only from provided context, cite sources.
- Persist messages to `ChatMessages` table.
- Temperature=0, explicit max tokens, retry logic.

### 7. Blazor UI (`Chat.razor`)
- Chat interface with message history, input box, send button.
- Stream LLM responses token-by-token.
- Display source citations below each assistant response.
- Session management (new chat / continue).

### 8. DI registration & Program.cs
- Register `IChatClient`, `IEmbeddingGenerator`, DbContext, services.
- Configure Blazor Server interactive mode.
- Health checks for OpenAI + Postgres.

### 9. Validation
- `dotnet build` clean with no warnings.
- Verify ingestion parses `product-guide.pdf`.
- Verify end-to-end chat flow.

## Key Guardrails
- Temperature = 0 for factual Q&A
- Pin model versions (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- Retry with exponential backoff on LLM calls
- Minimum similarity score on vector search
- Source attribution on every answer
- API keys from env vars / user-secrets only
- Streaming responses for UX

## Notes
- `Docs/` already contains `product-guide.pdf` — ingested on first run.
- Postgres + pgvector extension must be available; connection string in `appsettings.json`.
- OpenAI API key set via `AI__ApiKey` env var or `dotnet user-secrets`.

+### 4. PDF ingestion service (`DocIngestionService`)
+- Scan `Docs/` folder for PDFs on startup or on-demand.
+- Extract text with PdfPig, split into semantic chunks (~500 tokens, paragraph-aware).
+- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator`.
+- Upsert chunks into `DocumentChunks` table. Skip already-ingested files (content hash check).
+
+### 5. Vector search service (`VectorSearchService`)
+- Embed user query, cosine similarity search against `DocumentChunks`.
+- Return top-5 chunks above minimum relevance threshold (0.75).
+- Include source attribution (file name, chunk index).
+
+### 6. Chat service (`ChatService`)
+- RAG flow: embed query → retrieve context → build prompt → call LLM → stream answer.
+- System prompt: answer only from provided context, cite sources.
+- Persist messages to `ChatMessages` table.
+- Temperature=0, explicit max tokens, retry logic.

+### 7. Blazor UI (`Chat.razor`)
+- Chat interface with message history, input box, send button.
+- Stream LLM responses token-by-token.
+- Display source citations below each assistant response.

+### 8. DI registration & Program.cs
+- Register `IChatClient`, `IEmbeddingGenerator`, DbContext, services.
+- Configure Blazor Server interactive mode.
+- Health checks for OpenAI + Postgres.
+
+### 9. Validation
+- `dotnet build` clean with no warnings.
+- Verify ingestion parses `product-guide.pdf`.
+- Verify end-to-end chat flow.
+
+## Key Guardrails
+- Temperature = 0 for factual Q&A
+- Pin model versions (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
+- Retry with exponential backoff on LLM calls
+- Minimum similarity score on vector search
+- Source attribution on every answer
+- API keys from env vars / user-secrets only
+- Streaming responses for UX
